axis: ssot-drift detector — first run + state baseline
trigger: Parallel session committed c40b419e (audit_shim_ssot drift detector) — pick up cycle requires running it, surfacing findings, deciding fix vs audit-only.
verdict: audit-only — detector reports clean baseline; in-scope fix surface = ZERO; one defect found in detector's own --witness mode (deferred as toolchain-3, target file is parallel-session-locked).

detector.purpose: Catch the regression class that bit on 2026-04-25 e075b3e8: SSOT generator (tool/install_interp_shim.hexa) was deprecated for the mkdir-mutex/safe_hexa_launchd design, but the live gitignored artifact (build/hexa_interp) silently retained the bad pattern after a stage0->interp rename. Detector audits (live, ssot) pairs against known-bad and required-current pattern lists.

registered_pairs.rationale: live is gitignored bash shim, SSOT is iss_fallback_shim_content() generator

default.stdout_summary: audit-shim-ssot — registered pairs: 1 / [ok] build/hexa_interp <- tool/install_interp_shim.hexa / RESULT: clean (no drift in 1 pair(s))

selftest.tests_passing: 8/8 (T1 registry has 1 row, T2 row schema, T3 scan_pair clean, T4 scan_pair flags deprecated, T5 scan_pair flags missing-good, T6 strip_comments, T7 doc-only bad mention not flagged, T8 code bad pattern flagged)

witness.outcome: FAIL — witness defect under raw#9 OS-level lock posture
witness.stdout: [witness] BEGIN / backed up live shim -> /tmp/audit_shim_ssot.witness.backup / injected deprecated pattern (safe_hexa_launchd) into live shim as active code / [witness] audit run: drift_pairs=0 caught_pattern=no
witness.root_cause: build/hexa_interp has chflags uchg (immutable, set by raw#9 OS-level enforcement after install_interp_shim runs). The witness's safe_write_file() call at line 334 silently no-ops because the target is immutable, so the injection never lands -> _audit_all sees clean tree -> caught_pattern=no -> drift_pairs=0. Then the recovery `cp backup live_path` at line 352 also fails (uchg blocks overwrite) -> witness exits 3.

live_file_state.kind: bash text shim, fork-storm cap design (no mkdir mutex, no safe_hexa_launchd) — matches SSOT generator output

out_of_scope_detail.summary: --witness mode cannot inject into the live shim because raw#9 chflags-uchg has been applied; both injection write and restore cp fail silently. Detector's other modes (default/--json/--selftest) work correctly and the production drift-detection capability is intact — only the self-test witness is broken.
out_of_scope_detail.why_deferred: tool/audit_shim_ssot.hexa is parallel-session-locked per cycle constraints (the parallel session committed it 75 min ago and may iterate). Editing here would risk merge conflict.
out_of_scope_detail.proposed_fix: Witness should chflags nouchg before injection / re-chflags uchg after restore, OR detect uchg up-front and SKIP witness gracefully (return 0 with [witness] SKIP — uchg-locked by raw#9), OR run the injection on a temp copy and audit the copy. Recommend option 2 (skip + clear sentinel) for least intrusion.
out_of_scope_detail.follow_up_axis: omega-toolchain-3 (witness-uchg-coexistence) — defer until parallel session settles tool/audit_shim_ssot.hexa

deferred_residuals_observed_via_state_js.comment: Already cataloged and triaged by c40b419e author. No action required this cycle.

decision.rationale: Detector reports clean across default/--json/--selftest. The single non-clean signal (--witness exit 3) is a defect inside the parallel-session-locked detector file itself, not a real-world drift in any artifact. There is no actionable in-scope fix — the live shim and SSOT generator agree, and the detector's main job is working correctly.

untouched_locked_paths: self/native/native_gate.c (dirty in worktree from parallel session)
tool/audit_shim_ssot.hexa (committed 75 min ago by parallel session, may iterate)

headline: ω-toolchain-2: detector clean (1/1 pair OK, 8/8 selftest), surfaced 1 deferred follow-up (witness-mode breaks under raw#9 uchg) → toolchain-3.
